label: Check answers
description: Allow the user to review and confirm all collected information before a final, irreversible action.
purpose: Reduce submission errors and give users confidence before committing to an action.
use_when: Before any irreversible action (submit, pay, sign, issue credential).
After collecting multiple pieces of information across several pages.
do_not_use_when: The user has only answered a single question with no side effects.
The action can be easily undone after submission.

inputs.label: All collected data
inputs.description: All data gathered in prior steps, ready for review.

outputs.label: Confirmed data
outputs.description: User-confirmed data, ready for final submission.

variants.label: Read-only review
variants.description: User can only confirm, not amend inline. Use when all prior steps are accessible via back-navigation.

variants.label: Inline edit
variants.description: User can amend answers directly on the check-answers page without going back.
use_when: Form has few fields and inline editing does not create complexity.

ux_considerations.text: Allow users to edit any field without losing other answers.
Display data in plain language, not technical field IDs or codes.
Group related fields visually (e.g. personal info, address, payment).
For assisted or offline channels, read answers back to the user before confirming.

technical_notes.text: Do not re-validate server-side at this step — perform full validation only on final submission.
Mask sensitive data where full display is not required (e.g. partial card numbers, partial ID).
Audit log the confirmation action with timestamp and user reference.
